title: Hotel Key Management Procedures
trigger: Initiation of guest check-in or check-out process.

steps.title: Receive Guest Check-In Notification
steps.action: The front desk receives a notification from the PMS indicating a guest is ready to check-in. Verify the guest's reservation details and prepare the key card or physical key.

steps.title: Verify Guest Identity
steps.action: Request a valid form of identification from the guest. Ensure the ID matches the reservation details in the PMS.

steps.title: Encode Key Card
steps.action: Use the key card encoder to program the guest's room key. Ensure the key is set for the correct duration of stay.

steps.title: Issue Key to Guest
steps.action: Hand the encoded key card to the guest along with a welcome package. Provide any necessary instructions for using the key card.

steps.title: Record Key Issuance
steps.action: Log the issuance of the key in the PMS, noting the key number and the guest's details.

steps.title: Monitor Key Usage
steps.action: Regularly monitor key usage through the PMS to ensure security and address any anomalies.

steps.title: Resolve ID Mismatch
steps.action: If the guest's ID does not match the reservation, escalate the issue to the Front Desk Supervisor for resolution.

description: This playbook outlines the procedures for managing guest key issuance and control within a hotel setting in Egypt. It ensures secure and efficient handling of keys to enhance guest experience and operational security.
escalation_path: Front Desk Supervisor > Operations Manager > General Manager
